generatedSentence: The `oligarch` `influencer`, `vaguely` remembered Earth's `many` `restaurants` and `organic` `refrigerator` farms, now just `annotations` in a forgotten `republic`. His `rust`-eaten `locomotive` rumbled across the `geographic` expanses of `Mars`, guided by a `joystick` covered in `electrical` `emblems`, its `internal` `balance` `unreal`. He was `jacked` on `caffeine`, his `fingers` `twitch`ing, feeling `unworthy` as a former `sugardaddy` `trucker` and `windjammer` for hire, carrying `inventories` `legally` declared `incorrect` by the `picker`. Suddenly, a `berserker` `vampire` `knight`, with a `jeweled` `backpack` `bought` from an `archer`, gave a `summons`. `Everyone` `understand`s this `national` call; even the `chimpanzee` `wizard` who often took a `hiatus` from his `unassailable` tower, and the `grizzly` `raider` `nicknamed` "`Chicken`." The `mystery` was `linked` to `many` `ants` forming `kanji` characters on `cardboard` `tables`, emitting a `vector` `aura` of `dirty` `urine`. He tried to `unsay` his `drunken` past, but the `thunders` of `emergencies` `effused`. The `mammoth` task required `effortless` `gymnastics` to `unearth` the `bulbous` `oyster` that held the true `balance` of power. `Solar` flares, like `crayons` `flick`ering at `sunrise`, `heats` up the `wind`. A `dragonfly` `pickoff` was attempted by `dingos`, but the `trader` `bewitch`ed them with a `haiku` etched onto a `waterproof` `hamburger` wrapper. The air `twisted` with `vicious` `censorship` as the `konimeter` readings soared, revealing `secur`ity `emblems` that `upheave`d all `legitimate` `invitee`s. He had to `upload` the `cowry` shell codes, a `pickoff` maneuver that would `rack` the `weapons` of the `daffy` `windrow`. He mounted his `jetski`, `cinematic` `wind` whipping around him. This was the `climax` he'd always `understand` – a final, `secular` showdown against the forces that sought to control the `republic` with `vicious` `censorship`. His `fingers` `flick`ered over the controls, `effortless`ly guiding the craft, `linked` to his `internal` resolve. The `sunrise` painted the sky as he prepared to `unearth` the truth, ending the `hiatus` of their freedom.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a grizzled space trucker wearing a tattered, waterproof backpack and a silver, electrical emblem on his shoulder. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, seen from a low-angle medium shot, steers a rusty, locomotive-like space vehicle across a desolate Martian landscape, neon lights glowing from the control panel. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A dramatic, wide shot of a towering, jeweled vampire knight on a jetski, leading a charge of diverse, quirky characters (a chimpanzee wizard, a grizzly raider) towards the protagonist's vehicle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A close-up, eye-level shot of the protagonist's fingers effortlessly navigating a complex, glowing joystick, while a tiny, bulbous oyster pulsates on a holographic map beside him. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A high-angle long shot of the protagonist on his jetski, silhouetted against a brilliant, neon sunrise, leaving the damaged locomotive behind as he races towards a distant, futuristic city.

definitions.definition: An organic compound.
An organic food.
A living organism, as opposed to a robot or hologram.
Pertaining to or derived from living organisms.
Pertaining to an organ of the body of a living organism.
Relating to the compounds of carbon, relating to natural products.
Of food or food products, grown in an environment free from artificial agrichemicals, and possibly certified by a regulatory body.

Describing a form of social solidarity theorized by Emile Durkheim that is characterized by voluntary engagements in complex interdependencies for mutual benefit (such as business agreements), rather than mechanical solidarity, which depends on ascribed relations between people (as in a family or tribe).
Of a military unit or formation, or its elements, belonging to a permanent organization (in contrast to being temporarily attached).
Instrumental; acting as instruments of nature or of art to a certain destined function or end.
(of search results) Generated according to the ranking algorithms of a search engine, as opposed to paid placement by advertisers.
Developing in a gradual or natural fashion.
definitions.example: The writing of the script was an organic process.

definitions.definition: Harmonious; coherent; structured.
definitions.example: The production came together in an organic whole.

definitions.definition: An eating establishment in which diners are served food, usually by waiters at their tables but sometimes (as in a fast food restaurant) at a counter.
A household appliance used for keeping food fresh by refrigeration (short form fridge).
One who has a chilling influence.
A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives, rather than with a monarch or emperor; a country with no monarchy.
definitions.example: The United States is a republic; the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland is a constitutional monarchy.

definitions.definition: A state, which may or may not be a monarchy, in which the executive and legislative branches of government are separate.
One of the subdivisions constituting Russia. See oblast.
definitions.example: The Republic of Udmurtia is west of the Permian Oblast.

definitions.definition: Agent noun of pick; one who picks.
definitions.example: The apple picker climbed the tree.

definitions.definition: Any user interface control that selects something.
definitions.example: a date picker

definitions.definition: A machine for picking fibrous materials to pieces so as to loosen and separate the fibre.
The piece in a loom that strikes the end of the shuttle and impels it through the warp.
A priming wire for cleaning the vent, in ordnance.
(gold panning) A fragment of gold smaller than a nugget but large enough to be picked up.
One who removes defects from and finishes electrotype plates.
A pilferer.
In a vague manner.
definitions.example: He looked vaguely around the room, as if lost.

definitions.definition: Loosely; more or less; somewhat.
One who plays a wind instrument, especially a bugler in the army.
A sailing ship; especially a large, iron-hulled, square-rigged ship with three or more masts.
A member of the crew of a ship of this kind.
A windbag, a loquacious person.
A weatherproof jacket (windcheater).
A critical or explanatory commentary or analysis
A comment added to a text
The process of writing such comment or commentary
Metadata added to a document or program
Information relating to the genetic structure of sequences of bases
Any of various insects in the family Formicidae in the order Hymenoptera, typically living in large colonies composed almost entirely of flightless females.
A Web spider.
To rub insects, especially ants, on one's body, perhaps to control parasites or clean feathers.
A mechanical device consisting of a handgrip mounted on a base or pedestal and typically having one or more buttons, used to control an aircraft, computer or other equipment.
A penis.
To manoeuvre by means of a joystick.
To withdraw, retract (something said).
To not have said (since this is physically impossible, usually in the subjunctive).
definitions.example: I wish I could unsay that.

definitions.definition: A berserk (a crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy).
One who fights as if frenzied, like a berserker.
A type of von Neumann probe whose mission is to exterminate alien lifeforms.
A state in which opposing forces harmonise; equilibrium.
Mental equilibrium; mental health; calmness, a state of remaining clear-headed and unperturbed.
Something of equal weight used to provide equilibrium; counterweight.
definitions.example: Blair thought he could provide a useful balance to Bush's policies.

definitions.definition: A pair of scales.
Awareness of both viewpoints or matters; neutrality; rationality; objectivity.
The overall result of conflicting forces, opinions etc.; the influence which ultimately "weighs" more than others.
definitions.example: I think the balance of opinion is that we should get out while we're ahead.

definitions.definition: Apparent harmony in art (between differing colours, sounds, etc.).
A list accounting for the debits on one side, and for the credits on the other.
The result of such a procedure; the difference between credit and debit of an account.
definitions.example: I just need to nip to a bank and check my balance.

definitions.definition: A device used to regulate the speed of a watch, clock etc.
The remainder.
definitions.example: The balance of the agreement remains in effect.

definitions.definition: Libra.
To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
To make (concepts) agree.
To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
definitions.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

definitions.definition: To compare in relative force, importance, value, etc.; to estimate.
(dancing) To move toward, and then back from, reciprocally.
definitions.example: to balance partners

definitions.definition: To contract, as a sail, into a narrower compass.
definitions.example: to balance the boom mainsail

definitions.definition: To make the credits and debits of (an account) correspond.
definitions.example: This final payment, or credit, balances the account.

definitions.definition: To be in equilibrium.
To have matching credits and debits.
To weigh in a balance.
To hesitate or fluctuate.
As permitted by law; not contrary to law.
definitions.example: You can legally park in the lot on weekends without paying the fee, they won't ticket you.

definitions.definition: From a legal perspective.
definitions.example: Legally, I think you are covered, but there are angry guys with baseball bats outside.

definitions.definition: The power unit of a train that pulls the coaches or wagons.
A traction engine
A cheer characterized by a slow beginning and a progressive increase in speed
A country which drives the world economy by having a high level of imports. (i.e. The United States).
Of or relating to locomotion
Of or relating to the power unit of a train which does not carry passengers or freight itself
A blemish.
To spoil; to ruin; to scathe; to damage.
A small lake.
A multitude; a great aggregate; a mass of people; the generality; the common herd.
definitions.example: Democracy must balance the rights of the few against the will of the many.

definitions.definition: A considerable number.
A large number of; numerous.
An indefinite large number of people or things.
definitions.example: Many are called, but few are chosen.

definitions.definition: An electrical engineer.
Related to electricity (or electronics)
A representative symbol, such as a trademark or logo.
Something which represents a larger whole.
definitions.example: The rampant poverty in the ethnic slums was just an emblem of the group's disenfranchisement by the society as a whole.

definitions.definition: Inlay; inlaid or mosaic work; something ornamental inserted in a surface.
A picture accompanied with a motto, a set of verses, etc. intended as a moral lesson or meditation.
A man who spends money for the benefit of a relationship with an often younger romantic or sexual partner.
One who has done something offensive; a deceitful, dishonest, or disreputable person; a deceiver; a cheat.
A rogue; rascal; miscreant; a ne'er-do-well.
Dishonest; resulting from or inspired by dishonesty.
Pertaining to geography (or to geographics)
Determined by geography, as opposed to magnetic (i.e. North)
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: A person who or a thing which influences.
A member of an oligarchy; someone who is part of a small group that runs a country.
(especially Russia, USA, Europe, or China) A very rich person, particularly with political power; a plutocrat.
(cosmogony) A protoplanet formed during oligarchic accretion.
The deteriorated state of iron or steel as a result of moisture and oxidation.
definitions.example: The rust on my bicycle chain made cycling to work very dangerous.

definitions.definition: A similar substance based on another metal (usually with qualification, such as "copper rust").
definitions.example: aerugo. Green or blue-green copper rust; verdigris. (American Heritage Dictionary, 1973)

definitions.definition: A reddish-brown color.
A disease of plants caused by a reddish-brown fungus.
Damage caused to stamps and album pages by a fungal infection.
A light source consisting of a wick embedded in a solid, flammable substance such as wax, tallow, or paraffin.
The protruding, removable portion of a filter, particularly a water filter.
A unit of luminous intensity, now replaced by the SI unit candela.
A fast-growing, light-colored, upward-growing shoot on a pine tree in the spring. As growth slows in summer, the shoot darkens and is no longer conspicuous.
To observe the growth of an embryo inside (an egg), using a bright light source.
To dry greenware prior to beginning of the firing cycle, setting the kiln at 200° Celsius until all water is removed from the greenware.
To check an item (such as an envelope) by holding it between a light source and the eye.
A species of great ape in the genus Pan, native to Africa, and believed by biologists to be the closest extant relative to humans.
A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
Set with jewels
A powered watercraft with a seat and handlebars in a style similar to a motorbike.
To ride on a jetski.
Such a file transfer.
To transfer data to a computer on a network, especially to a server on the Internet.
definitions.example: You will have to convert your photograph to a jpg format if you wish to upload it.

definitions.definition: Liquid waste consisting of water, salts and urea, which is made in the kidneys, stored in the bladder, then released through the urethra.
To urinate.
A person born to a legally married couple.
To make legitimate, lawful, or valid; especially, to put in the position or state of a legitimate person before the law, by legal means.
In accordance with the law or established legal forms and requirements.
Conforming to known principles, or established or accepted rules or standards; valid.
definitions.example: legitimate reasoning; a legitimate standard or method

definitions.definition: Authentic, real, genuine.
definitions.example: legitimate poems of Chaucer; legitimate inscriptions

definitions.definition: Lawfully begotten, i.e., born to a legally married couple.
Relating to hereditary rights.
Thermal energy.
definitions.example: This furnace puts out 5000 BTUs of heat.   That engine is really throwing off some heat.   Removal of heat from the liquid caused it to turn into a solid.

definitions.definition: The condition or quality of being hot.
definitions.example: Stay out of the heat of the sun!

definitions.definition: An attribute of a spice that causes a burning sensation in the mouth.
definitions.example: The chili sauce gave the dish heat.

definitions.definition: A period of intensity, particularly of emotion.
definitions.example: It's easy to make bad decisions in the heat of the moment.

definitions.definition: An undesirable amount of attention.
definitions.example: The heat from her family after her DUI arrest was unbearable.

definitions.definition: The police.
definitions.example: The heat! Scram!

definitions.definition: One or more firearms.
A fastball.
definitions.example: The catcher called for the heat, high and tight.

definitions.definition: A condition where a mammal is aroused sexually or where it is especially fertile and therefore eager to mate.
definitions.example: The male canines were attracted by the female in heat.

definitions.definition: A preliminary race, used to determine the participants in a final race
definitions.example: The runner had high hopes, but was out of contention after the first heat.

definitions.definition: One cycle of bringing metal to maximum temperature and working it until it is too cool to work further.
definitions.example: I can make a scroll like that in a single heat.

definitions.definition: A hot spell.
definitions.example: The children stayed indoors during this year's summer heat.

definitions.definition: Heating system; a system that raises the temperature of a room or building.
definitions.example: I'm freezing; could you turn on the heat?

definitions.definition: The output of a heating system.
definitions.example: During the power outage we had no heat because the controls are electric.   Older folks like more heat than the young.

definitions.definition: In omegaverse fiction, a cyclical period in which alphas and omegas experience an intense, sometimes irresistible biological urge to mate.
To cause an increase in temperature of (an object or space); to cause to become hot (often with "up").
definitions.example: I'll heat up the water.

definitions.definition: To become hotter.
definitions.example: There's a pot of soup heating on the stove.

definitions.definition: To excite or make hot by action or emotion; to make feverish.
To excite ardour in; to rouse to action; to excite to excess; to inflame, as the passions.
To arouse, to excite (sexually).
definitions.example: The massage heated her up.

definitions.definition: (plural noun) A period of hot weather.
definitions.example: Everyone suffered during the heats of July.

definitions.definition: A slender jointed extremity of the human hand, exclusive of the thumb.
definitions.example: Humans have two hands and ten fingers. Each hand has one thumb and four fingers.

definitions.definition: Similar or similar-looking extremities in other animals, particularly:
Something similar in shape to the human finger, particularly:
Something similarly extending, from a larger body, particularly:
definitions.example: a finger of land; a finger of smoke

definitions.definition: Something similar in function or agency to the human finger, with regard to touching, grasping, or pointing.
Various units of measure based or notionally based on the adult human finger, particularly
A part of a glove intended to cover a finger.
Skill in the use of the fingers, as in playing upon a musical instrument.
Someone skilled in the use of their fingers, a pickpocket.
A person.
(especially in the phrase 'give someone the finger') An obscene or insulting gesture made by raising one's middle finger towards someone with the palm of one's hand facing inwards.
To identify or point out. Also put the finger on. To report to or identify for the authorities, rat on, rat out, squeal on, tattle on, turn in.
To poke, probe, feel, or fondle with a finger or fingers.
To use the fingers to penetrate and sexually stimulate one's own or another person's vagina or anus; to fingerbang
To use specified finger positions in producing notes on a musical instrument.
To provide instructions in written music as to which fingers are to be used to produce particular notes or passages.
To query (a user's status) using the Finger protocol.
To steal; to purloin.
To execute, as any delicate work.
A short, quick movement, especially a brush, sweep, or flip.
definitions.example: He removed the speck of dust with a flick of his finger.

definitions.definition: A motion picture; (in plural, usually preceded by "the") movie theater, cinema.
definitions.example: My all-time favorite flick is "Gone with the Wind."

definitions.definition: A cut that lands with the point, often involving a whip of the foible of the blade to strike at a concealed target.
A powerful underarm volley shot.
The act of pressing a place on a touch screen device.
A flitch.
definitions.example: a flick of bacon

definitions.definition: A unit of time, equal to 1/705,600,000 of a second
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.
definitions.example: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.

definitions.definition: Someone or something with an obscure or puzzling nature.
definitions.example: That man is a mystery.

definitions.definition: A secret or mystical meaning.
A religious truth not understandable by the application of human reason alone (without divine aid).
(obsolete outside Eastern Orthodoxy) A sacrament.
(chiefly in plural) A secret religious celebration, admission to which was usually through initiation.
definitions.example: the Eleusinian mysteries

definitions.definition: A particular event or series of events in the life of Christ.
definitions.example: The second decade of the Rosary concerns the Sorrowful mysteries, such as the crucifixion and the crowning with thorns.

definitions.definition: A craft, art or trade; specifically a guild of craftsmen.
Without effort.
To emit; to give off
To gush; to be excitedly talkative and enthusiastic about something
To pour out like a stream or freely; to cause to exude; to shed.
To leak out through a small hole
A call to do something, especially to come.
A notice summoning someone to appear in court, as a defendant, juror or witness.
A demand for surrender.
To serve someone with a summons.
One who earns a living by trading goods or securities.
The loud rumbling, cracking, or crashing sound caused by expansion of rapidly heated air around a lightning bolt.
definitions.example: Thunder is preceded by lightning.

definitions.definition: A deep, rumbling noise resembling thunder.
definitions.example: Off in the distance, he heard the thunder of hoofbeats, signalling a stampede.

definitions.definition: An alarming or startling threat or denunciation.
The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.
The spotlight.
definitions.example: Shortly after I announced my pregnancy, he stole my thunder with his news of landing his dream job.

definitions.definition: To produce thunder; to sound, rattle, or roar, as a discharge of atmospheric electricity; often used impersonally.
definitions.example: It thundered continuously.

definitions.definition: To make a noise like thunder.
definitions.example: The train thundered along the tracks.

definitions.definition: To talk with a loud, threatening voice.
To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.
definitions.example: "Get back to work at once!", he thundered.

definitions.definition: To produce something with incredible power
An instrument for measuring the concentration of dust.
Inside of something
definitions.example: We saw the internal compartments.

definitions.definition: Within the body
definitions.example: Her bleeding was internal.

definitions.definition: Concerned with the domestic affairs of a nation, state or other political community.
definitions.example: The nation suffered from internal conflicts.

definitions.definition: Concerned with the non-public affairs of a company or other organisation
definitions.example: An internal investigation was conducted.

definitions.definition: Not correct; erroneous or wrong.
definitions.example: He gave an incorrect answer to a simple question.

definitions.definition: Faulty or defective.
definitions.example: The computer crashed due to incorrect programming.

definitions.definition: Inappropriate or improper.
definitions.example: He was sacked because of his incorrect behaviour towards his secretary.

definitions.definition: A person who is invited into or onto someone else's premises
A series of one or more shelves, stacked one above the other
Any of various kinds of frame for holding luggage or other objects on a vehicle or vessel.
A device, incorporating a ratchet, used to torture victims by stretching them beyond their natural limits.
A piece or frame of wood, having several sheaves, through which the running rigging passes.
A bunk.
(by extension) Sleep.
A distaff.
A bar with teeth on its face or edge, to work with those of a gearwheel, pinion#, or worm, which is to drive or be driven by it.
A bar with teeth on its face or edge, to work with a pawl as a ratchet allowing movement in one direction only, used for example in a handbrake or crossbow.
A cranequin, a mechanism including a rack, pinion and pawl, providing both mechanical advantage and a ratchet, used to bend and cock a crossbow.
A set of antlers (as on deer, moose or elk).
A cut of meat involving several adjacent ribs.
definitions.example: I bought a rack of lamb at the butcher's yesterday.

definitions.definition: A hollow triangle used for aligning the balls at the start of a game.
A woman's breasts.
A friction device for abseiling, consisting of a frame with five or more metal bars, around which the rope is threaded.

definitions.definition: A climber's set of equipment for setting up protection and belays, consisting of runners, slings, carabiners, nuts, Friends, etc.
definitions.example: I used almost a full rack on the second pitch.

definitions.definition: A grate on which bacon is laid.
That which is extorted; exaction.
A set with a distributive binary operation whose result is unique.
A thousand pounds (£1,000), especially such proceeds of crime
To place in or hang on a rack.
To torture (someone) on the rack.
To cause (someone) to suffer pain.
To stretch or strain; to harass, or oppress by extortion.
To put the balls into the triangular rack and set them in place on the table.
To strike a male in the testicles.
To (manually) load (a round of ammunition) from the magazine or belt into firing position in an automatic or semiautomatic firearm.
To move the slide bar on a shotgun in order to chamber the next round.
To wash (metals, ore, etc.) on a rack.
To bind together, as two ropes, with cross turns of yarn, marline, etc.
(structural engineering) Tending to shear a structure (that is, force it to move in different directions at different points).
definitions.example: Post-and-lintel construction racks easily.

definitions.definition: A small gastropod (Monetaria moneta, syn. Cypraea moneta) common in the Indian Ocean; its shell.
(by extension) Any gastropod of the genus Cypraea; its shell.
(by extension) Any gastropod of the family Cypraeidae; its shell.
An alkaloid, C8H10N4O2, found naturally in tea and coffee plants which acts as a mild stimulant on the central nervous system.
A stick of colored chalk or wax used for drawing.
A colored pencil, a colouring pencil
A crayon drawing, or a drawing with colored lines.
A pencil of carbon used in producing electric light.
An informal map of a proposed rail route.
A play in which a pitcher throws a live ball to a fielder so that the fielder can tag out a baserunner who has moved away from the base
Someone, usually male, who uses (or has skill with) magic, mystic items, and magical and mystical practices.
One who is especially skilled or unusually talented in a particular field.
definitions.example: He was a financial wizard, capable of predicting the movements of the stock markets.

definitions.definition: A computer program or script used to simplify complex operations, often for an inexperienced user.
definitions.example: Use the "Add Network Connection" wizard to connect to a network in a series of simple steps.

definitions.definition: One of the administrators of a multi-user dungeon.
A wise man; a sage.
An adult virgin over the age of 30.
To practice wizardry.
To conjure.
Fine, superb (originally RAF slang).
A row of cut grain or hay allowed to dry in a field.
A line of leaves etc heaped up by the wind.
A similar streak of seaweed etc on the surface of the sea formed by Langmuir circulation.
A line of snow or gravel left behind by the edge of a snowplow’s or grader’s blade.
The green border of a field, dug up in order to carry the earth on other land to mend it.
To arrange (e.g. new-made hay) in lines or windrows.
One who shoots an arrow from a bow or a bolt from a crossbow.
The bishop in chess.
Something, such as a belief, that cannot be assailed.
Secure against attack; impregnable.
(by extension) Undeniable, incontestable or incontrovertible.
definitions.example: She won the debate with her unassailable logic.

definitions.definition: Fake; not real
Very impressive; amazing; unbelievable; incredible; larger or more fantastic than typical of real life.
definitions.example: I just had an unreal hamburger.

definitions.definition: Having the shape of or resembling a bulb, bloated.
(of a person) Overweight and round in shape.
Growing from a bulb or producing bulbs.
To connect two or more things.
(of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.
definitions.example: My homepage links to my wife's.

definitions.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.
definitions.example: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.

definitions.definition: To post a hyperlink to.
definitions.example: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

definitions.definition: To demonstrate a correlation between two things.
To combine objects generated by a compiler into a single executable.
To skip or trip along smartly; to go quickly.
Connected, either with links, or as if with links
Having links (between modules, records etc)
A Japanese poem in three lines, the first and last consisting of five morae, and the second consisting of seven morae, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
A three-line poem in any language, with five syllables in the first and last lines and seven syllables in the second, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
definitions.example: Haiku, a poem

definitions.definition: A hot sandwich consisting of a patty of cooked ground beef or a meat substitute, in a sliced bun, sometimes also containing salad vegetables, condiments, or both.
The patty used in such a sandwich.
Ground beef, especially that intended to be made into hamburgers.
(somewhat crude) An animal or human, or the flesh thereof, that has been badly injured as a result of an accident or conflict.

definitions.definition: A hamburger button.
A gap in a series, making it incomplete.
An interruption, break or pause.
An unexpected break from work.
A gap in geological strata.
An opening in an organ.

definitions.definition: A syllable break between two vowels, without an intervening consonant. (Compare diphthong.)
definitions.example: Words like reality and naïve contain vowels in hiatus.

definitions.definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
Drunk, in the state of intoxication after having drunk an alcoholic beverage

definitions.definition: Given to habitual excessive use of alcohol.
Characterized by or resulting from drunkenness.
definitions.example: a drunken display of crude exuberance

definitions.definition: Saturated with liquid
Somewhat mad or eccentric.
An insect of the suborder Epiprocta or, more strictly, the infraorder Anisoptera, having four long transparent wings held perpendicular to a long body when perched.

Any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus, of large, usually hairy, elephant-like mammals with long curved tusks and an inclined back, which became extinct with the last retreat of ice age glaciers during the late Pleistocene period, and are known from fossils, frozen carcasses, and Paleolithic cave paintings found in North America and Eurasia.
A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.
A secular ecclesiastic, or one not bound by monastic rules.
A church official whose functions are confined to the vocal department of the choir.
A layman, as distinguished from a clergyman.
Not specifically religious; lay or civil, as opposed to clerical.
Temporal; worldly, or otherwise not based on something timeless.
Not bound by the vows of a monastic order.

definitions.definition: Happening once in an age or century.
definitions.example: The secular games of ancient Rome were held to mark the end of a saeculum and the beginning of the next.

definitions.definition: Continuing over a long period of time, long-term.
definitions.example: The long-term growth in population and income accounts for most secular trends in economic phenomena.

definitions.definition: Centuries-old, ancient.
Relating to long-term non-periodic irregularities, especially in planetary motion or magnetic field.
(atomic physics) Unperturbed over time.
The time of day when the sun appears above the eastern horizon.

definitions.definition: The change in color of the sky at dawn.

definitions.definition: Any great awakening.
definitions.example: It was the sunrise of her spirit.

definitions.definition: A subject of a nation.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A tournament in which participants from all over the nation compete.
definitions.example: After winning the regional tournament, the team advanced to the nationals.

definitions.definition: Of or having to do with a nation.
(by extension) Of or having to do with a country (sovereign state).
definitions.example: Import tariffs were raised for the national interest.

definitions.definition: Furniture with a top surface to accommodate a variety of uses.
A two-dimensional presentation of data.
The top of a stringed instrument, particularly a member of the violin family: the side of the instrument against which the strings vibrate.
One half of a backgammon board, which is divided into the inner and outer table.
The flat topmost facet of a cut diamond.
To tabulate; to put into a table or grid.

definitions.definition: To supply (a guest, client etc.) with food at a table; to feed.
To delineate; to represent, as in a picture; to depict.
(non-US) To put on the table of a commission or legislative assembly; to propose for formal discussion or consideration, to put on the agenda.
To remove from the agenda, to postpone dealing with; to shelve (to indefinitely postpone consideration or discussion of something).

definitions.definition: To join (pieces of timber) together using coaks.
To put on a table.
To make board hems in the skirts and bottoms of (sails) in order to strengthen them in the part attached to the bolt-rope.
Backgammon.
The halves or quarters of a backgammon board.
Any backgammon-like board game, played on a board with two rows of 12 vertical markings called "points".
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A minor nobleman with an honourable military rank who had served as a page and squire.
(by extension) An armored and mounted warrior of the Middle Ages.

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.

A sport involving the performance of sequences of movements requiring physical strength, flexibility, and kinesthetic awareness.

definitions.definition: Complex intellectual or artistic exercises or feats of physical agility.

definitions.definition: A grizzly bear.

definitions.definition: In hydraulic mining, a grating used to catch and throw out large stones from the sluices.
Grey-haired, greyish.
One who engages in a raid; a plunderer.

A special forces operative; a commando.
A warship which is light, maneuverable, and fast-moving.
A person who uncovers evidence of improper behavior within governmental or private organizations.
A domestic fowl, Gallus gallus, especially when young.
The meat from this bird eaten as food.
The young of any bird; a chick.
A coward.
A young or inexperienced person.
A young, attractive, slim man, usually having little body hair; compare chickenhawk.
The game of dare.
A simple dance in which the movements of a chicken are imitated.
Cowardly.

definitions.definition: Real or perceived movement of atmospheric air usually caused by convection or differences in air pressure.

definitions.definition: Air artificially put in motion by any force or action.

definitions.definition: The ability to breathe easily.

definitions.definition: News of an event, especially by hearsay or gossip. (Used with catch, often in the past tense.)

definitions.definition: One of the five basic elements in Indian and Japanese models of the Classical elements).
Flatus.

definitions.definition: Breath modulated by the respiratory and vocal organs, or by an instrument.
The woodwind section of an orchestra. Occasionally also used to include the brass section.
A direction from which the wind may blow; a point of the compass; especially, one of the cardinal points, which are often called the "four winds".
Types of playing-tile in the game of mah-jongg, named after the four winds.
A disease of sheep, in which the intestines are distended with air, or rather affected with a violent inflammation. It occurs immediately after shearing.
Mere breath or talk; empty effort; idle words.
A bird, the dotterel.
The region of the solar plexus, where a blow may paralyze the diaphragm and cause temporary loss of breath or other injury.
To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.

To turn a boat or ship around, so that the wind strikes it on the opposite side.
To expose to the wind; to winnow; to ventilate.
To perceive or follow by scent.

To turn a windmill so that its sails face into the wind.
A substance or preparation for rendering cloth, leather, etc., impervious to water.
Cloth made waterproof, or any article made of such cloth, or of other waterproof material, as rubber; especially, an outer garment made of such material.
To make waterproof or water-resistant.
Unaffected by water.
Made of or covered with material that doesn't allow water in.
Incapable of failing; unassailable.

An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.
To raise using a jack.

definitions.definition: To raise or increase.

definitions.definition: To produce by freeze distillation; to distil (an alcoholic beverage) by freezing it and removing the ice (which is water), leaving the alcohol (which remains liquid).

definitions.definition: To dance by moving the torso forward and backward in a rippling motion.
To hit (the ball) hard; especially, to hit (the ball) out of the field, producing a home run.
High on drugs or stimulants.

definitions.definition: Strong and/or muscled.

definitions.definition: An inadequate person.
Not worthy; lacking value or merit; worthless.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

definitions.definition: To heave or lift up; raise up or aloft.

definitions.definition: To dig up.
To cast a spell upon.
To fascinate or charm.

definitions.definition: To astonish, amaze.
To make (something) dirty.
To stain or tarnish (somebody) with dishonor.
To debase by distorting the real nature of (something).
To become soiled.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.

definitions.definition: That makes one unclean; corrupting, infecting.
definitions.example: Don't put that in your mouth, dear. It's dirty.

definitions.definition: Morally unclean; obscene or indecent, especially sexually.

definitions.definition: Dishonourable; violating accepted standards or rules.

definitions.definition: Corrupt, illegal, or improper.

definitions.definition: Out of tune.

definitions.definition: Of color, discolored by impurities.
definitions.example: The old flag was a dirty white.

definitions.definition: Containing data needing to be written back to memory or disk.

definitions.definition: Carrying illegal drugs among one's possessions or inside of one's bloodstream.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially in conjunction with "great".
definitions.example: He lives in a dirty great mansion.

definitions.definition: Sleety; gusty; stormy.

definitions.definition: Of an alcoholic beverage, especially a cocktail or mixed drink: served with the juice of olives.

definitions.definition: Of food, indulgent in an unhealthy way.
definitions.example: The waiter served dirty burgers to the customers.

definitions.definition: In a dirty manner.

definitions.definition: Canis lupus dingo, a wild dog native to Australia.
Every person.
A situation which poses an immediate risk and which requires urgent attention.

definitions.definition: The department of a hospital that treats emergencies.
An individual brought in at short notice to replace a member of staff, a player in a sporting team, etc.
The quality of being emergent; sudden or unexpected appearance; an unforeseen occurrence.
To give a nickname to (a person or thing).
To turn the ends of something, usually thread, rope etc., in opposite directions, often using force.
To join together by twining one part around another.
To contort; to writhe; to complicate; to crook spirally; to convolve.
To wreathe; to wind; to encircle; to unite by intertexture of parts.
To wind into; to insinuate.

definitions.definition: To turn a knob etc.
To distort or change the truth or meaning of words when repeating.
To form a twist (in any of the above noun meanings).
To injure (a body part) by bending it in the wrong direction.
(of a path) To wind; to follow a bendy or wavy course; to have many bends.
To cause to rotate.
To dance the twist (a type of dance characterised by twisting one's hips).
To coax.
In the game of blackjack (pontoon or twenty-one), to be dealt another card.
Contorted.
Wound spirally.
Mentally disturbed or unsound.

definitions.definition: Under the influence of multiple intoxicants, usually alcohol and marijuana.
Couch grass (Elymus repens; a species of grass, often considered as a weed)
The system of writing Japanese using Chinese characters.
Any individual Chinese character as used in the Japanese language.
definitions.example: I know about a thousand kanji.

definitions.definition: Any of certain marine bivalve mollusks, especially those of the family Ostreidae (the true oysters), usually found adhering to rocks or other fixed objects in shallow water along the seacoasts, or in brackish water in the mouth of rivers.
The delicate morsel of dark meat contained in a small cavity of the bone on each side of the lower part of the back of a fowl.
A pale beige color tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
(by analogy) A person who keeps secrets.
A shoplifter.
To fish for oysters.
Of a pale beige colour tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
The use of state or group power to control freedom of expression or press, such as passing laws to prevent media from being published or propagated.
The role of the censor (magistrate) in Ancient Rome.
A wood-based material resembling heavy paper, used in the manufacture of boxes, cartons and signs.
Made of or resembling cardboard; flat or flavorless.
A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
Violent, destructive and cruel.
Savage and aggressive.
Pertaining to vice; characterised by immorality or depravity.
An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.

definitions.definition: An instrument or other means of harming or exerting control over another.
definitions.example: Money is the main weapon of modern oligarchs.

definitions.definition: A tool of any kind.
definitions.example: Choose your weapon.

definitions.definition: An idiot, an oaf, a fool, a tool; a contemptible or incompetent person.

A knapsack, sometimes mounted on a light frame, but always supported by straps, worn on a person’s back for the purpose of carrying things, especially when hiking, or on a student's back when carrying books.
A similarly placed item containing a parachute or other life-support equipment.
To hike and camp overnight in backcountry with one's gear carried in a backpack
To engage in low-cost, generally urban, travel with minimal luggage and frugal accommodations
To place or carry (an item or items) in a backpack
To obtain (something) in exchange for money or goods

definitions.definition: To obtain by some sacrifice.

definitions.definition: To bribe.

definitions.definition: To be equivalent to in value.

definitions.definition: To accept as true; to believe

definitions.definition: To make a purchase or purchases, to treat (for a meal)
definitions.example: Let's go out for dinner. I'm buying.

definitions.definition: To make a bluff, usually a large one.
